s_specific_solution.core_mechanism: Weiss Risk Ratings / Safe Money Investor Service / Contrarian Crash Strategy: Dr. Martin D. Weiss provides the ONLY 100 percent objective, conflict-of-interest-free ratings on 7,000 stocks and 9,500 mutual funds. The US General Accounting Office (GAO) compared Weiss' ratings to Wall Street's - the GAO report is a 'glowing tribute to Weiss,' according to Barron's. Jack Anderson reports Weiss ratings are 'THREE TIMES MORE ACCURATE' than anyone else. Forbes named Dr. Weiss 'MR. INDEPENDENCE.' The Wall Street Journal calls him 'FEISTY.' The New York Times says he was 'the first to see the dangers and say so unambiguously.' We use state-of-the-art network with the power of a Cray Super Computer to scan the US and world economies for opportunity and danger. Of the 6,100 stocks we rated in the crash of April 2000, the average stock fell 9%. But stocks with a Weiss Risk Rating of 'Very Risky' dropped nearly twice as much - 16%. Stocks that earned our best rating fared 50% better than average. According to the Los Angeles Times, 'Of the 62 publicly traded companies to file for bankruptcy protection in the first quarter, Weiss said it rated 36 of them at least three months before their Chapter 11 or Chapter 7 filing, and 34 of those got weak or very weak investment ratings of D-plus to E-minus.'
mechanism_proof_points: US General Accounting Office (GAO) study - glowing tribute
Jack Anderson - THREE TIMES MORE ACCURATE than competitors
NY Attorney General investigation validates Weiss warnings
Barron's: GAO report is glowing tribute
unique_differentiators: ONLY 100% objective, conflict-of-interest-free rating service. GAO proved 3X more accurate than Wall Street. Predicted Enron, WorldCom, Global Crossing, Kmart bankruptcies months before they happened. No company pays for ratings. Not in anyone's back pocket. Independent since 1971.

lead.full_text: Dear Fellow Investor: If you ignore the enclosed book, you do so at your own peril. You see, this isn't the first time I've written you. I've sent surprising - but remarkably accurate - forecasts to you and to my own clients many times in the past several months. For example... In November of 1999 I warned, 'The high-tech bubble is ready to blow wide open!' 'No sector of the market is more wildly valued than Internet stocks,' I said. 'These 'no-earnings' stocks could fall 50%-75% in a very short period of time.' Result: Within 120 days, the great Nasdaq crash of 2000 had begun - a bloody collapse that would hammer the average stock down 41%. Investors who ignored my advice lost nearly $2 trillion over the next 5 months. But those who accepted my help piled up profits of up to 416% while tech stocks crashed. In July 2000, with the market already severely down, I cautioned, 'We now have proof-concrete, irrefutable, and damning evidence - that a tech-stock collapse is in the making AGAIN!' Result: In September, less than a month after my second warning, the next big crash began, smashing the Nasdaq from 4206 to 1619. ANOTHER $3 TRILLION was vaporized. But my clients absolutely cleaned up with profits of up to 929%! In March 2001, after a 7-month slide in tech stocks, I saw a bear-market rally ahead: 'Take 35% and 159% profits now,' I wrote. 'And prepare your portfolio for a potential bear-market rally.' Result: The great spring rally came, driving Nasdaq stocks up 44%, and my subscribers used it to clean out the last stocks in their portfolio.
lead.hook_sentence: If you ignore the enclosed book, you do so at your own peril.
lead.transition_to_body: Now, in 2003, the worst panic of all is about to impact the U.S. economy and stock markets.

schema_analysis.expectation_violated: Investment newsletters make vague predictions and cherry-pick results. But Weiss provides specific dates, specific warnings, specific outcomes with exact percentages - and third-party verification.
schema_analysis.whitespace_territory: Extreme precision in predictions (within 30-120 days) and documented massive returns (929%) while market collapsed - proves author has genuine edge

three_part_structure.what_they_think_problem_is: Some bad apples (Enron, WorldCom) cooked books. Tech bubble popped. Markets corrected. Worst is over. Brokers say buy - time to recover.
three_part_structure.what_real_problem_is: Wall Street is SYSTEMATICALLY corrupt. Merrill Lynch and 5 other major firms were caught giving 'buy' recommendations while internal emails called same stocks 'garbage.' NY Attorney General exposed tainted ratings. 32% of publicly-traded companies use suspect accounting. Seven massive crises are converging: (1) Great Stock Panic worse than 1929, (2) Debt Disaster ($4.7T corporate debt), (3) Banking Crisis (bad loans exploding), (4) Real Estate Collapse (bubble 3X larger than tech), (5) Asian Meltdown (Japan's $1T bad debt), (6) Pension Crisis ($2.4T shortfall), (7) Energy Crisis redux.
three_part_structure.why_nothing_worked: You trusted corrupt brokers whose tainted ratings caused $5 trillion in losses. Merrill Lynch emails proved they knowingly recommended 'garbage' stocks. Wall Street's incentive is deal flow and commissions, not your returns. Bush tax cut is back-loaded 5-10 years. Greenspan's rate cuts don't work in deflation. Stocks still massively overvalued - S&P at 38X earnings needs to fall to 7-8X (historical bottom). Real estate bubble is 3X bigger than tech bubble and will crash 30-40%. Japan's collapse will crash US stocks another 20-25%.

specimens.quote: In November of 1999 I warned, 'The high-tech bubble is ready to blow wide open!' Result: Within 120 days, the great Nasdaq crash of 2000 had begun... Investors who ignored my advice lost nearly $2 trillion over the next 5 months. But those who accepted my help piled up profits of up to 416% while tech stocks crashed.

In July 2000, with the market already severely down, I cautioned, 'We now have proof-concrete, irrefutable, and damning evidence - that a tech-stock collapse is in the making AGAIN!' Result: In September... the next big crash began, smashing the Nasdaq from 4206 to 1619. ANOTHER $3 TRILLION was vaporized. But my clients absolutely cleaned up with profits of up to 929%!

We Banked Profits Of Up To 929% Even While Stocks Were Crashing: Nasdaq-100: 416% in 23 Days, 242% in 68 Days, 213% in 19 Days, 212% in 53 Days. CSFB Technology Index: 929% in 42 Days. Gold: 525% in 20 Days, 350% in 62 Days.
THE GENERAL ACCOUNTING OFFICE of the US CONGRESS (GAO) heralded the fact that Weiss beat its closest competitor by a factor of 3 to 1 in forecasting future financial troubles.
Investigative journalist JACK ANDERSON calls Dr. Weiss GUTSY and reports that his insurance ratings are 'THREE TIMES MORE ACCURATE' than anyone else in the business.

According to the LOS ANGELES TIMES, 'Of the 62 publicly traded companies to file for bankruptcy protection in the first quarter, Weiss said it rated 36 of them at least three months before their Chapter 11 or Chapter 7 filing, and 34 of those got weak or very weak investment ratings of D-plus to E-minus... the lowest-rated shares, including US Airways Group Inc., JDS Uniphase Corp. and Xerox Corp., have lost an average of 33.1%, while the highest-rated stocks are up an average of 14.9%'

Merrill Lynch has been slammed by the New York attorney general because of tainted ratings - bold-faced lies that persuaded investors to buy stocks while Merrill's own internal e-mails clearly recognized those same stocks were pure garbage. Five other major Wall Street firms have also been subpoenaed for the same kind of shenanigans. These lies have already caused average investors losses of over $5 trillion.

Saved me a fortune - 'You saved me! I got your advice to sell ALL my tech stocks in late 1999. Every one said you were crazy but I followed you because your arguments were so persuasive. You saved me a fortune!' - Subscriber, Apple Valley, CA

Saved $42,000 - 'I had $42,000 invested in an annuity at Executive Life. I saw your low rating and got out before the company failed. Thank you for saving me from what could have been a real disaster.' - M.A.B., Riverview, MI

FORBES named Dr. Weiss 'MR. INDEPENDENCE.' THE WALL STREET JOURNAL calls Martin D. Weiss 'FEISTY.' BARRON'S agrees, saying the GAO report is a 'glowing tribute to Weiss.' THE NEW YORK TIMES says Dr. Weiss was 'the first to see the dangers and say so unambiguously.'

Merrill called stocks 'garbage' privately while telling you to buy publicly. This isn't speculation - it's prosecuted fact. Your losses weren't paranoia - you were systematically conned.

fssit_candidates.statement: These lies have already caused average investors losses of over $5 trillion. And yet, most Wall Street firms are still continuing to give millions of investors the same kind of tainted 'buy' recommendations RIGHT NOW!
fssit_candidates.past_explaining_power: Quantifies the crime ($5 trillion stolen) and confirms your suspicion that it's STILL HAPPENING. You're not crazy to distrust the recovery narrative - they're STILL lying with the same tainted ratings that caused $5T in losses.

fssit_candidates.past_explaining_power: Explains why recovery keeps disappointing and why 'this time is different' feeling is accurate. For first time in decades, NO major economy is healthy enough to pull others up. Your instinct that something fundamental is broken is correct.
